output: Batch 5 complete. `_handle_test_receipt_ambiguous` now sends a recipient-facing clarification message on the first ambiguous reply and preserves the two-strike escalation behavior. The handler uses the LLM-provided `clarification_reply` when present, otherwise it sends the default `confirmed/yes/not received` prompt. Verification passed for `tests/test_admin_payments.py` and for the full repository suite.

task_updates.executor_notes: Updated `_handle_test_receipt_ambiguous` at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:617-646` so that after incrementing the ambiguous reply counter, it sends a recipient-facing in-thread clarification when the post-increment count is `< 2`. When `clarification_reply` is provided, the handler sends `f"<@{recipient_user_id}> {clarification_reply}"`; otherwise it sends the default clarification asking for `confirmed`/`yes` or `not received`. The existing `>= 2` escalation still delegates directly to `_handle_test_receipt_negative(updated_intent)` with no `clarification_reply` forwarding. Updated the existing ambiguous-path regression test at `tests/test_admin_payments.py:1527-1530` to assert the first clarification message is sent. Verification: `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py --tb=no -q --no-header` passed; `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` passed with `201 passed, 4 warnings`.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed. `_handle_test_receipt_ambiguous` now posts a recipient-facing in-thread message on the first ambiguous reply at `src/features/admin_chat/admin_chat_cog.py:634-645`, uses the LLM-supplied `clarification_reply` when present, falls back to the default `confirmed/yes/not received` clarification otherwise, and leaves the `>= 2` branch untouched apart from delegating to `_handle_test_receipt_negative(updated_intent)` without forwarding `clarification_reply`.
